$comment: Hand-authored peer-set taxonomy. See docs/concepts/peer-sets.md for the rules. Empty file-level `sources[]` is the canonical signal for hand-authored content per ADR-0002 — tier classifications are constitutional/statutory facts, not fetched artifacts; per-tier `authority` carries the legal citation. Adding a tier is purely additive; broadening `topic-catalogue.schema.json`'s `peer_set_default` enum to reference it is a separate minor bump there.

tiers.label: General-category states
tiers.definition: The 18 states that were never on the pre-14th-FC special-category list. Defined by exclusion: every `kind: state` in states.json minus the `special_category` legacy 11 (less J&K which became a UT in 2019, leaving 10 of the 11 still as states; 28 − 10 = 18). The 14th Finance Commission (2015) folded the 30%-special-plan-assistance regime into the 42% horizontal devolution pool; 'general' here is therefore a fiscal residual, not a constitutional category. Used as the default peer set for fiscal indicators where comparing across the asymmetric centre-transfer regime would be misleading (e.g. outstanding-debt-pct-GSDP).
tiers.notes: Andhra Pradesh (S01) is included here. The 2014 Reorganisation Act promised special-category status; the Centre never notified it. We follow notification, not promise.

tiers.label: Special-category states (legacy)
tiers.definition: The 11 states historically conferred Special-Category Status (SCS) by successive Finance Commissions and the National Development Council from 1969 onwards: Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, Tripura, Jammu & Kashmir (now a UT, retained here for fiscal-history continuity), Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand. The 14th Finance Commission (2015) discontinued SCS as a fiscal devolution category, but the Centre still applies the 90:10 Centrally-Sponsored Scheme funding ratio to these 11 (vs 60:40 for general-category states). The category is dead in FC arithmetic, alive in CSS arithmetic; ignoring it would misread MGNREGA / PMAY / JJM transfer comparisons.
tiers.authority: Pre-14th-FC NDC resolutions (1969 onwards); 14th Finance Commission Report (2015) for discontinuation; current operative use: Ministry of Finance Rationalisation of CSS OM (2021)
tiers.notes: U08 (Jammu & Kashmir) appears here AND in `ut_legislature` — this dual membership is intentional. The fiscal history the indicator is comparing predates the 2019 reorganisation.

tiers.label: North-Eastern states (NEH Council)
tiers.definition: The 8 states constituting the North-Eastern Council under the North-Eastern Council Act 1971 (as amended): Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim (added 2002), Tripura. Members of the Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (MDoNER) funding window. Cited by FC reports as a distinct grouping for NEH-specific grants and 10% gross-budgetary-support earmark.
tiers.authority: North-Eastern Council Act 1971; Ministry of DoNER mandate (2001)

tiers.label: Himalayan states
tiers.definition: States and UTs whose territory is dominated by the Himalayan range, used as a peer set for topographical / agro-climatic comparisons (forest cover, hydropower potential, climate vulnerability). Includes the eastern Himalayan stretch (Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, Tripura), the western Himalayan stretch (Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand), and the trans-Himalayan UTs (Jammu & Kashmir, Ladakh). Excludes Assam (Brahmaputra plain, not Himalayan). Distinct from `neh` (which is a statutory FC funding window with different membership and different policy citations) per panel decision — set near-equality is not grounds for merging when the two tiers are cited by different authorities.

tiers.label: Union Territories with legislature
tiers.definition: Union Territories with an elected legislative assembly under Article 239A: Puducherry (Government of Union Territories Act 1963) and Jammu & Kashmir (Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act 2019, with Art. 239A extended). NCT-Delhi is governed by Article 239AA (a sui generis regime with police, land and public order reserved to the Centre) and is intentionally NOT in this tier — it has its own singleton tier `nct_delhi` to prevent category-error comparisons against Puducherry.
tiers.authority: Article 239A; Government of Union Territories Act 1963; Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act 2019

tiers.label: Union Territories without legislature
tiers.definition: Union Territories administered directly by the President through an Administrator under Article 239: Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Chandigarh, Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu, Lakshadweep, Ladakh. No elected legislature; budget approved by Parliament.
tiers.authority: Article 239; Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act 2019 (for Ladakh)

tiers.label: NCT of Delhi
tiers.definition: Sui generis singleton tier for the National Capital Territory of Delhi under Article 239AA. Delhi has an elected Legislative Assembly with circumscribed powers — police, public order and land are reserved to the Centre via the Lieutenant Governor (per the 2021 GNCTD Amendment Act). Singleton because comparing Delhi against either Puducherry (`ut_legislature`) or full states is a category error: Delhi has VAT/GST on a metropolitan consumption base that Puducherry lacks, but lacks land-use authority that any state has.
tiers.authority: Article 239AA; Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi Act 1991; GNCTD (Amendment) Act 2021

tiers.label: FC-XV horizontal devolution share (quintiles)
tiers.definition: States binned into quintiles by their share of the divisible-pool horizontal devolution under the 15th Finance Commission award (2021–2026). The Centre's own answer to 'who needs more' — the single most honest fiscal peer set, refreshed each FC cycle. Members[] is empty in v1.0 pending recon of FC-XV Final Report Volume I, Chapter 8 (Annex 5 has the per-state shares); populated in a follow-up commit. Catalogue may declare this as a `peer_set_default`; the consumer-side filter must gracefully degrade to 'all' when members[] is empty.
tiers.authority: 15th Finance Commission Final Report (2020), Volume I, Chapter 8
tiers.notes: RECON PENDING. Do not populate from memory — the per-state shares are tabular and easily mis-transcribed. Open a separate commit that cites the Annex 5 table directly. Refresh on FC-XVI publication (~2025).

tiers.label: Coastal states and UTs
tiers.definition: States and UTs with a coastline, per the Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) notification regime administered by MoEFCC. 9 mainland states + 4 UTs. Useful peer set for fisheries, ports, blue-economy, cyclone-exposure indicators. Not a fiscal peer set.
tiers.authority: Coastal Regulation Zone Notification (MoEFCC); Maritime Zones of India Act 1976

tiers.label: Landlocked states and UTs
tiers.definition: Complement of `coastal_states`: states and UTs without a coastline. Useful peer set for logistics-cost, inland-trade, freight-corridor indicators.

tiers.label: States with Article 371 special provisions
tiers.definition: States with special constitutional provisions under Article 371 and 371A–371J. Each state's provision addresses a distinct local concern: Maharashtra/Gujarat (371: Vidarbha/Saurashtra special boards), Nagaland (371A: customary law), Assam (371B: tribal areas committee), Manipur (371C: hill areas committee), Andhra Pradesh + Telangana (371D: shared post-bifurcation employment/education zones), Sikkim (371F: pre-merger laws preserved), Mizoram (371G: customary law), Arunachal Pradesh (371H: governor's special responsibility for law and order), Goa (371I: assembly size floor), Karnataka (371J: Hyderabad-Karnataka region). Useful peer set for land-rights, autonomous-council, mining-royalty, employment-quota indicators.
tiers.authority: Constitution of India, Articles 371 and 371A through 371J
